hypothesis: Network density materially below breakeven/ceiling
evidence: Disclosed: 1,093 OPD (Q4 FY26) vs. stated 2,000+ ceiling (~55% utilization)

hypothesis: Loss concentrated in a minority of stores
evidence: Disclosed: only ~25% of stores profitable as of Q2 FY26

hypothesis: New stores take 6-12mo to mature, dragging blended margin
evidence: No public store-cohort/vintage data available

hypothesis: Megapods improve throughput/margin via SKU breadth
evidence: Qualitative only — SKU count, ~50% of net adds

hypothesis: Density alone explains the margin gap across companies
evidence: Zepto: highest density (~2,140), near-worst loss. Blinkit: mid density (~1,357), best loss by far — business model likely dominates

hypothesis: Density predicts margin within a single network
verdict: Methodology demonstrated on calibrated proxy data
evidence: R²=0.42 on n=1143 simulated stores; regression x-intercept ≈1,619 OPD — overstates the ~1,225 real-data breakeven (Redseer)

hypothesis: Instamart leads on AOV — a durable margin advantage
evidence: Gross AOV ₹700 vs ₹525 (Blinkit, net) vs ₹387 (Zepto); net-to-net Instamart's lead narrows (~₹485 vs ₹525)

hypothesis: Instamart's deficit is frequency/MTU, not basket size
evidence: 13.3M vs Blinkit's 27.2M MTU (both disclosed)

hypothesis: Non-grocery mix expansion raises AOV/margin per order
evidence: Both series rose together; only 2 time points, no causal proof

hypothesis: Reduced promo intensity traded volume for economics
evidence: First-ever sequential GOV decline (-0.7%) alongside +65bps margin gain

hypothesis: Advertising is currently driving take-rate expansion
evidence: Take rate softened 21.1% -> ~19.5% (Q4 FY26, JM Financial) — the opposite of an ads-led rise

hypothesis: Instamart's ad revenue lags peers, leaving headroom
evidence: Zepto discloses ₹1,636cr; Instamart doesn't disclose at all

hypothesis: Take rate trails Blinkit's 20-22% guidance band
evidence: ~19.5% (Q4 FY26, JM Financial), below Blinkit's stated 20-22% band

hypothesis: Non-grocery/private-label mix lifts ad + product margin
evidence: No public data isolates ad-margin contribution specifically

hypothesis: Instamart losing share of incremental category growth
evidence: Order share fell 34.3% -> 24.5% -> 20.9%, FY24-FY26 (Zepto UDRHP)

hypothesis: Metro saturation caps orders/store and AOV via demand-splitting
evidence: Same underlying phenomenon as H3.1/H3.4 — no independent data

hypothesis: New entrants (Amazon Now, Flipkart Minutes) fragmenting demand
evidence: Flipkart Minutes ~800 stores, +~100/month; Amazon targeting 100 cities, 1,000+ MFCs

hypothesis: Zepto's higher throughput despite lower AOV proves frequency beats ticket size
evidence: Zepto's density lead hasn't produced better unit economics than Instamart's — Blinkit's model, not density, looks decisive

hypothesis: Last-mile cost is large and density-sensitive
evidence: Blinkit ₹55/order, -14% YoY; no Instamart/Zepto comparator exists

hypothesis: Tier-2 expansion raises blended delivery cost
evidence: No public city-tier cost breakdown for any player

hypothesis: Marketplace model prevents Instamart from matching Blinkit's replenishment efficiency
evidence: 90% Blinkit inventory-led NOV; ~100bps EBITDA accretion + ~300bps gross-margin lift (Blinkit-observed, Eternal CFO). Blocked for Instamart by FDI rules (inventory model needs IOCC status); enabling vote fell 2.65pp short of 75%. Secondary to density (06a/06d)

hypothesis: Rider/regulatory cost inflation is a forward risk
evidence: Forward-looking policy risk by construction, not a backward-looking metric
